---
applyTo: "assignments/**/*.md"
---

# Assignment Markdown Standards

Use these rules for every assignment markdown file under `assignments/`.

## 1. File and Template Requirements

- Every assignment must be a `README.md` file in its assignment folder.
- Follow the structure in [`templates/assignment-template.md`](../../templates/assignment-template.md).
- Keep all required sections from the template. Do not remove, rename, or reorder required headers.
- Keep the template icon style in headings (`📘`, `🎯`, `📝`, `🛠️`).

## 2. Required Section Structure

Each assignment must include the following sections in order:

1. `# 📘 Assignment: [Assignment Title]`
2. `## 🎯 Objective`
3. `## 📝 Tasks`
4. One or more task blocks, each with:
   - `### 🛠️ [Task Title]`
   - `#### Description`
   - `#### Requirements`

## 3. Content Quality Standards

- Title: Use a short, descriptive assignment name.
- Objective: Write 1-2 sentences explaining what students will learn or build.
- Task titles: Use specific, action-oriented wording.
- Description: Clearly explain what the student needs to do.
- Requirements:
  - Start with `Completed program should:`
  - Use bullet points
  - Make outcomes specific and measurable
- Add short examples in code blocks when they improve clarity.

## 4. Educational Tone and Scope

- Keep language student-friendly, clear, and encouraging.
- Keep content learning-focused with appropriate difficulty for students.
- Do not add extra sections unless the assignment explicitly requires them.
